meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use AI-driven debugging to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate guide for founders using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship fast.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of portfolio stall your momentum. By leveraging AI-driven debugging, you can rapidly identify architectural bottlenecks and fix complex bugs in legacy or prototype code. This approach transforms debugging into an iterative feature-building cycle, proving your technical execution capability to investors and customers alike.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a founder's greatest adversary. You have the vision, but your lack of a robust portfolio creates a massive trust deficit with potential stakeholders. You are stuck in a loop of 'what could be' rather than 'what is shipping,' watching competitors with inferior products gain traction simply because they have a history of execution.

But the rules of the game have shifted. You no longer need years of legacy experience to build high-performance SaaS. By mastering AI-assisted debugging, you turn every error message into a masterclass in architecture, allowing you to build, refactor, and stabilize complex systems at 10x the velocity of a traditional developer.

steps.title: Isolate the Architectural Bottleneck
steps.description: Identify the specific module causing the most friction in your MVP. Use AI to analyze the dependency graph rather than guessing where the bug originates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for cyclic dependencies and memory leaks in the primary data processing pipeline. Provide a high-level architectural summary.

steps.title: Generate Context-Aware Debugging Logs
steps.description: Inject intelligent logging into your application to capture state transitions, making it easier for Claude to diagnose intermittent failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current error handling middleware to include structured, context-rich logging that captures user state and input parameters before any crash.

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed your logs and stack traces into the AI to map the exact execution path that leads to the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the attached stack trace and log output. Identify the race condition in the asynchronous state management logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review these logs: $(cat logs/error.log) and identify the root cause'

steps.title: Iterative Fix and Regression Testing
steps.description: Apply the fix and immediately generate a unit test to ensure the bug never returns. This builds your 'proof of quality' portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a patch for the identified race condition and generate a Jest test suite that verifies the fix under high concurrency.

steps.title: Documenting the Technical Solution
steps.description: Transform your debugging session into a 'Technical Case Study' for your portfolio, detailing the problem, your methodology, and the outcome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert the logic changes made in this session into a professional engineering blog post that highlights the problem-solving methodology used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Summarize these changes into a technical case study for my portfolio'

pro_tips: Treat every bug report as a feature request; if the code is failing, it's usually because the abstraction is too complex.
Use Claude Code to generate 'self-healing' code by adding automated retry logic to your most fragile API endpoints.
Always commit your AI-generated fixes with descriptive tags like 'fix: architecture-optimization' to create a searchable history for your portfolio.
Don't just fix the code; ask the AI to explain the trade-offs of the chosen solution to deepen your own engineering intuition.
Maintain a 'bug-to-feature' ratio in your commit history to demonstrate to investors that you prioritize stability alongside speed.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is stable, deploy it to a staging environment and use Claude to perform a security audit before your public launch.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical leverage to overcome the empty portfolio trap. Stop building in the dark and start shipping with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
